gaps: MISSING DIMENSION — Sandboxing / permission / tool-authorization model. None of the five surveys treats the agent's execution-safety surface (filesystem allowlist, bash command interception, approval gates, plan-mode guard, auto-generated-file guard). Yet oh-my-pi ships approval.ts, plan-mode-guard.ts, auto-generated-guard.ts, bash-interceptor.ts, bash-command-fixup.ts in packages/coding-agent/src/tools/. For a 'quality-per-dollar' coding harness that will run model-generated bash/edits, the permission/sandbox design is a first-order security and correctness dimension and is entirely absent from the research.

MISSING DIMENSION — Eval / benchmarking harness for measuring quality-per-dollar itself. The project's stated north star is QUALITY-PER-DOLLAR, but no survey covers how Misul would MEASURE it. oh-my-pi already ships eval.ts, eval-backends.ts, eval-render.ts; pi-mono ships a full provider test matrix (cross-provider-handoff.test.ts etc.). The skills survey mentions ponytail's promptfoo benchmark only in passing. There is no dimension on SWE-bench/Terminal-Bench harnessing, cost-tracking instrumentation, or per-model quality-per-dollar measurement methodology — the single most important thing for the stated goal.

UNCHECKED ADJACENT TOOLING — The MCP survey recommends forking external code-search MCP servers (codebase-memory-mcp, code-graph-mcp, mcp-ripgrep) and browser MCP servers (fast-playwright-mcp, chrome-devtools-mcp) WITHOUT noting that oh-my-pi already ships in-harness equivalents: search-tool-bm25.ts (BM25 code search), ast-grep.ts (AST structural search), find.ts, browser.ts (built-in browser tool), fetch.ts. The 'what to fork' analysis never asks 'what does the base harness already provide,' which is the first question for a fork. This is a major completeness omission that likely changes the MCP recommendation.

MISSING DIMENSION — Hindsight memory system depth. oh-my-pi ships an entire memory toolchain (memory-retain.ts, memory-recall.ts, memory-reflect.ts, memory-edit.ts, memory-render.ts) and docs/memory.md describes a two-phase extraction/consolidation pipeline. The system-prompt survey mentions this in one finding but no dimension evaluates memory architecture as a quality-per-dollar lever in its own right (it is referenced as a dependency for recursive-research and as a system-prompt sub-topic, never analyzed directly).

MISSING DIMENSION — Provider rate-limit / fallback / retry / cost-routing orchestration. The provider survey covers auth and per-model wire formats but not the operational layer: how the harness handles 429s, provider outages, automatic failover between OpenRouter/OpenCode-Go/direct, or cost-aware routing (cheap model for tool calls, expensive for reasoning). The reasoning survey alludes to model-swapping between turns via turn-snapshot, but no dimension treats routing/fallback as a system, despite it being central to quality-per-dollar.

INCOMPLETE — Snapcompact strategy underexamined. oh-my-pi ships a dedicated packages/snapcompact/src/snapcompact.ts (deterministic bitmap-frame compaction, no model call). The system-prompt survey lists it as one of five strategies in a single clause but never evaluates its cost/quality tradeoff, its vision-model dependency, or whether it is production-viable — despite it being a zero-token-cost compaction lever directly relevant to the north star.

INCOMPLETE — The skills survey omits oh-my-pi's own learn.ts / manage-skill.ts / bash-skill-urls.ts machinery. It evaluates external skills to bundle but never documents how Pi/oh-my-pi actually load, discover, and execute skills at runtime (the skill:// URL mechanism, manage-skill tool, learn tool), which determines whether any recommended external skill is even portable.

UNVERIFIED COST ARITHMETIC — The provider survey's claim 'DeepSeek V4 Flash ... about 158K requests/month at typical coding prompt sizes before hitting $60 monthly cap' is an unsupported derived figure with no stated prompt-size assumption. The actual model entry (models.generated.ts line 8580) is input $0.14/M, output $0.28/M, but '158K requests' depends entirely on an undisclosed token-per-request assumption and is not reproducible as stated.
unverifiedClaims: CROSS-REPO LAUNDERING (confirmed false as attributed) — The system-prompt survey states pi-mono's compaction threshold 'defaults to contextWindow - max(15% of contextWindow, reserveTokens)' and cites pi-mono's packages/agent/src/harness/compaction/compaction.ts. The actual shipping code at compaction.ts line 198 is `contextTokens > contextWindow - settings.reserveTokens` (flat reserveTokens=16384, no 15%/max() term). The '15% of contextWindow' formula appears ONLY in oh-my-pi's docs/compaction.md line 413, a different repo. The VERDICT rated this finding 'supported' and repeated the formula without catching the misattribution.

REPO-PATH CONFLATION — The system-prompt survey and verdict cite pi-mono compaction files at packages/agent/src/harness/compaction/. oh-my-pi's docs/compaction.md lists them at packages/agent/src/compaction/ (no harness/ segment). The two repos have different layouts and different threshold logic; the survey treats oh-my-pi doc behavior as if it were pi-mono source behavior.

MODEL COUNT — Provider survey claims opencode-go has '14 model entries' and lists 'Kimi K2.6/K2.7'. Direct count of models.generated.ts lines 8568-8802 yields exactly 13 distinct model IDs, and the K2.7 entry is 'kimi-k2.7-code', not 'kimi-k2.7'. The verdict acknowledged 13 but still mislabeled the original as '13 model entries' while the survey said 14; the model name is wrong in both.

REASONING SURVEY SELF-CONTRADICTION ON GROK-4.3 — The reasoning survey claims grok-4.3 'is not in models.generated.ts.' It is, at line 16452 (reasoning:true, no thinkingLevelMap). The verdict refuted this but then asserted the openai path 'sends reasoning_effort directly, which is correct for xAI' — contradicted by the SAME file: detectCompat sets supportsReasoningEffort = !isGrok (line 1140-1141) = false for xai, and every reasoning_effort branch (lines 565/585/610/620/623) gates on compat.supportsReasoningEffort. Net effect: reasoning_effort is NEVER sent for grok-4.3 as shipped. This is a real shipping bug neither survey nor verdict states cleanly.

PRICING — Anthropic survey's '$25-$82.50/MTok for Opus 4.8' was flagged by the verdict as wrong ($25 standard / $50 fast), but the corrected figure itself rests on a single vendor pricing page and a date-sensitive 'fast mode' that is not verifiable from the local clones. The pricing claims across the reasoning dimension are all single-source and date-sensitive; treat as needsVerification rather than resolved.

BENCHMARK NUMBERS REMAIN SINGLE-SOURCE — Across the MCP survey, every performance multiplier (zavora 939x frontmost-app, 31x clipboard; fast-playwright 70-95% token reduction; codebase-memory 99% / arXiv 10x; lightpanda 9x/16x) traces to a single vendor README or vendor blog. The verdicts correctly downgraded several to 'overstated,' but the netRecommendation still leans on these directional claims to pick winners. No independent replication exists for any browser/computer-use/code-search performance figure.

OAUTH ENFORCEMENT TIMELINE — The provider verdict 'refutes' the OAuth path as broken since April 2026 based entirely on external web sources (pi-mono issues, kersai.com, TechCrunch) that cannot be verified from the local clone. The CODE-level claims (stealth headers, version 2.1.75, tool remapping) ARE verified in anthropic.ts lines 72-109. The functional-status claim is unverifiable here and rests on time-sensitive third-party reporting.

STAR COUNTS — All GitHub star figures (ponytail 24.1k, taste-skill 45.2k, superpowers 226.4k/230k) are point-in-time, single-source, and self-evidently volatile (the verdict itself notes skillsllm showed 18.9k vs claimed 24.1k for ponytail). None are load-bearing for an engineering decision and should not be cited as evidence of quality.
contradictions: The system-prompt finding is rated 'supported' in VERDICTS while simultaneously containing a fabricated formula (15% threshold) that the actual cited pi-mono source does not implement. A finding cannot be both 'supported' and rest on code behavior that the cited file contradicts.

The reasoning dimension verdict says grok-4.3's openai path 'sends reasoning_effort directly, which is correct' AND says supportsReasoningEffort=false for isGrok in the same entry. These cannot both be true: if supportsReasoningEffort is false, the reasoning_effort branch is never entered, so nothing is sent.

The MCP survey's netRecommendation tells Misul to fork external code-search and browser MCP servers, while the oh-my-pi clone it is forking already ships search-tool-bm25.ts, ast-grep.ts, find.ts, and browser.ts. The 'fork external tooling' recommendation contradicts the fact that the base harness already provides the same capability class — a tension the survey never reconciles.

Provider survey says opencode-go uses 'OPENCODE_API_KEY (same as opencode provider)' and treats this as a clean reuse, but env-api-keys.ts lines 97-98 show BOTH opencode and opencode-go map to the same OPENCODE_API_KEY — meaning a user cannot hold separate credentials/quotas for the two providers simultaneously. The survey frames shared-key as a feature; for the per-user ToS-compliance model the verdict demands, shared keys are a constraint, not a convenience.

Reasoning survey claims Pi 'does not currently set thinkingFormat=openai for xAI models that expose reasoning_effort.' But detectCompat sets thinkingFormat to 'openai' as the default fallback for xai (line 1158), so xai DOES get thinkingFormat='openai' — the actual problem is supportsReasoningEffort=false, not the thinkingFormat. The survey misdiagnosed the mechanism.
recommendedFollowups: Re-audit the compaction-threshold claim end to end: confirm pi-mono shouldCompact() is flat (contextWindow - reserveTokens) and document that the 15%/max() formula is an oh-my-pi-only fork behavior in docs/compaction.md line 413. Correct every survey/verdict reference that attributes the 15% formula to pi-mono source.

File the grok-4.3 reasoning gap as a concrete defect: grok-4.3 has reasoning:true but supportsReasoningEffort resolves false (isGrok branch), so no reasoning_effort is ever sent. Decide whether xAI's actual API wants a top-level reasoning_effort (then add a compat override) or whether isGrok should be removed from the supportsReasoningEffort exclusion list. Add a test in the provider matrix.

Add a dedicated SANDBOX/PERMISSION dimension: audit approval.ts, bash-interceptor.ts, plan-mode-guard.ts, auto-generated-guard.ts in oh-my-pi and map the threat model (command injection via model output, path-escape, destructive git ops). This is the highest-value missing dimension for a harness that executes model-generated commands.

Add a QUALITY-PER-DOLLAR MEASUREMENT dimension: inventory oh-my-pi's eval.ts/eval-backends.ts and pi-mono's provider test matrix; specify how Misul will benchmark candidate model+effort+skill combinations on a fixed task suite with cost tracking. Without this, every 'quality-per-dollar' recommendation in the bundle is unfalsifiable.

Before adopting any external code-search or browser MCP, do a build-vs-fork comparison against oh-my-pi's shipped search-tool-bm25.ts, ast-grep.ts, and browser.ts. Quantify what the MCP servers add over the in-harness tools; the current MCP recommendation may be redundant with the base.

Reconcile the opencode-go inventory: lock the model list to the 13 verified IDs (correct 'kimi-k2.7' to 'kimi-k2.7-code', note minimax-m3/qwen3.7-max/qwen3.7-plus use anthropic-messages at /zen/go without /v1), and document that kimi-k2.6 + the deepseek pair have null thinkingLevelMaps for minimal/low/medium (thinking effectively off at those tiers).

Re-derive the DeepSeek V4 Flash cost projection with an explicit stated token-per-request assumption, or remove the '158K requests/month' figure. As written it is a round, unsupported number that fails the project's own skepticism rule.

Verify the OAuth-path functional status against a primary source or empirically, not via third-party blogs. If carrying the OAuth flow into the fork, gate it behind an explicit deprecation/legal warning and ensure the API-key path is the default, per the verdict's own recommendation.
